safest_plan.entry_basis: zero_live grade (2025-05-23), '4.10 HTF demand zone'; confirmed on chart as the deep 0.5-fib demand box labelled 4.10$

targets.basis: zero_live target; on chart the red 0.705-fib band labelled 'Most important level here' (5.66$)
zero_live target; on chart the upper red 0.886-fib zone labelled 6.22$

safest_plan.stop_basis: below the 4.10 demand zone / 0.5-fib floor (estimated). The source's stated 'below 3D break of structure' sits near 4.80, which is above the 4.10 entry and cannot act as its stop

stance_by_source.summary: A conditional buy: wait for price to drop into the 4.10 demand zone, then look for 5.66 and 6.22; for now just watching how price reacts in the retracement zone.

conflicts: The extracted text calls 4.10 the 'common support + demand zone', but the chart shows two separate floors: a shallower green 'Common support' around 4.6-4.7 and a deeper demand zone at 4.10 (0.5 fib). They are distinct buy zones, not one.

The stated stop 'below 3D break of structure' sits near 4.80 on the chart, which is above the 4.10 entry, so it cannot be the stop for that entry; a stop below the 4.10 zone (~3.95, estimated) is used instead.
This is a single, educational, year-old call (graded 5/10, 'partial'), so conviction is low even though there is no source-vs-source disagreement on direction.
plain_english: Only one source covers Polkadot here, and it is an old educational chart that mainly says 'wait and watch'. The plan is to buy lower, ideally near 4.10 where there is a strong support zone, then aim for 5.66 and 6.22, with the trade cut if price breaks well below 4.10. Right now the price near 4.95 is above that buy zone and the source is only observing how it behaves, so there is no clean entry yet. Wait for a pullback into support rather than chasing it here.
